title: Face Recognition Smart Door Lock
tips: Optimize local face recognition by compiling models using ESP-WHO framework for high-speed PSRAM execution.
Place a flyback diode (1N4007) across the solenoid coils to catch back-EMF spikes when switching the lock OFF.

title: Smart Attendance using AI Vision
tips: Verify lighting conditions. Dim lighting increases image noise, lowering classification model confidence thresholds.
Assign static IP configurations to the ESP32-CAM to prevent socket connection timeouts on local servers.

title: Edge AI Object Detection Node
connections: Camera Module connected via ribbon cable to CSI slot
Google Coral TPU plugged into USB 3.0 port (blue port)
code:
# Python script running TensorFlow Lite with Coral Edge TPU delegate
import time
import cv2
from pycoral.utils import edgetpu
from pycoral.adapters import common

# Load model compiled for Edge TPU
interpreter = edgetpu.make_interpreter('ssd_mobilenet_v2_coco_quant_postprocess_edgetpu.tflite')
interpreter.allocate_tensors()

cap = cv2.VideoCapture(0)
while cap.isOpened():
    ret, frame = cap.read()
    # Resize and run model
    # Display bounding box output on screen
    if cv2.waitKey(1) & 0xFF == ord('q'):
        break
tips: Google Coral TPU accelerates inference by converting float models to quantized 8-bit integer formats.
Ensure the Coral USB device is plugged into a USB 3.0 port (blue socket). USB 2.0 ports cannot supply sufficient bandwidth, causing dropouts.

title: TinyML IMU Gesture Classifier
connections: USB Cable → PC (Model inputs read from onboard LSM9DS1 IMU sensor)
tips: Use Edge Impulse to capture raw IMU datasets, label gesture movements, and download the optimized C++ source code.
Ensure the board is mounted firmly to your device or arm to prevent sensor vibration from generating noisy spikes.

title: AI Edge Traffic Monitoring Node
connections: Camera Module plugged into interface pins
tips: Deploy models on ESP32-S3 using the vector instruction extensions (ESP-NN) to increase model speed up to 3x.
Mount camera lenses high to reduce occlusion overlap between vehicles.

title: TinyML Plant Disease Detector
connections: ST7789 LCD SPI: MOSI → GPIO 13, SCLK → GPIO 12, CS → GPIO 11, DC → GPIO 10
tips: Crop leaf regions to focus model classifications on leaf spots instead of noisy background details.
Train classification models with leaf variations across multiple growth phases to prevent model overfitting.
